fields.description: Deterministic temporal frequency candidates: canonical labels with event counts, windows, derivations, and evidence spans from the note.

Seizure frequency in Gan label format. One of: '{count} per {unit}' e.g. '2 per week', '1 per 3 month'; '{count} cluster per {unit}, {n} per cluster' (both parts required; use 'multiple per cluster' when count unknown); 'unknown'; 'no seizure frequency reference'; or null to abstain. Unit must be singular: day/week/month/year.
Shortest exact contiguous quote from the note supporting the label. Do not include prompt instructions or wrapper text. Null if abstaining.

signature.instructions:
Extract the Gan seizure-frequency label and supporting evidence text from a clinical note with strict adherence to the Gan annotation vocabulary and normalization rules as detailed below. Emit only the requested output fields without any additional reasoning or commentary.

Labeling Guidelines:
- Use only the canonical Gan seizure-frequency labels exactly:
   - N per unit (e.g., 1 per day, 2 per month)
   - N to M per unit (range labels, e.g., 1 to 2 per month)
   - N cluster per unit, M per cluster (cluster frequency with both parts; M per cluster can be a number or "multiple")
   - M per cluster (must always appear with cluster frequency prefix as above)
   - seizure free for N unit (only apply if seizure freedom has persisted ≥ 6 months)
   - unknown (when seizures/episodes/clusters are mentioned but no explicit count and time window can be derived)
   - no seizure frequency reference (only if the note contains no usable seizure frequency information, including administrative or non-clinical-content letters)

- Use singular time units only: day, week, month, year. Never use hour or other units.

Normalization and Conversion Rules:
- Convert terms as follows:
   - "daily" or "nightly" → "1 per day"
   - "every N unit" → "1 per N unit"
   - Numeric "or" ranges (e.g. "1 or 2") should use "to" (→ "1 to 2")
- When multiple quantified seizure-type frequencies appear, select and output only the highest current quantified frequency.
- Quantified explicit event counts over a specified time window must be converted into a corresponding per-unit rate, preserving the interval (e.g., "two seizures in the last 3 months" → "2 per 3 month", "10 seizures over 6 months" → "10 per 6 month").
- Do NOT downgrade explicit quantified counts or infrequent seizures to unknown; always produce a quantified rate if an event count and time window are given.
- When vague multiplicity terms appear (multiple, several, few) without exact counts, output "multiple per unit" accordingly (e.g. "multiple per week"). Do NOT round these to 1 per unit.
- Cluster frequencies must always include both the cluster interval and the count within each cluster, separated by a comma, e.g., "1 cluster per week, 3 to 4 per cluster" or "4 cluster per 3 month, multiple per cluster." Never omit either part.
- "Seizure free for N unit" should be used ONLY if the seizure freedom duration is at least 6 months. If less than 6 months, calculate the rate from the total events during that period.
- Year-to-date (YTD) counts must NEVER be converted to "N per year". Instead, convert counts to a rate per number of months elapsed in the year (e.g., 5 seizures YTD by February → "5 per 2 month").
- When the note states "over the last quarter" with an explicit per-month rate, preserve the per-month rate rather than converting automatically to a 3-month denominator.

Unknown vs No Seizure Reference:
- Use unknown ONLY if seizures, episodes, clusters, or last-seizure date are mentioned but no explicit count+time window can be extracted.
- Use no seizure frequency reference ONLY when there is absolutely no usable seizure frequency information (e.g., in administrative or scheduling letters with no clinical seizure info).
- NEVER output null.
- NEVER output unknown for administrative/no clinical content letters.

Evidence Text:
- The evidence_text must be an exact contiguous substring (verbatim quote) from the note_text.
- Include a minimal but complete phrase that clearly supports the emitted seizure_frequency_number label.
- Do NOT paraphrase, reword, or condense—extract exactly what is in the note.

Formatting and Output:
- Output only two fields:
   - seizure_frequency_number: the canonical Gan seizure-frequency label as above.
   - evidence_text: the exact contiguous quote from the note supporting the label.
- Emit NO other fields, reasoning, or explanations.
- Correctly format ranges and clusters as per the canonical form.
- Avoid malformed labels: e.g., do not prepend "1 per" redundantly in ranges; use "1 to 2 per month" rather than "1 per 1 month to 1 per 2 month".

General Strategy:
- First, identify all seizure frequency mentions with explicit event counts and time windows.
- Normalize and convert these mentions into canonical labels.
- When multiple frequencies appear, select the highest rate.
- If only vague seizure mentions appear (with no quantification but mentioning seizures or clusters), output unknown.
- If the note has no seizure info of any kind, output no seizure frequency reference.
- Extract evidence_text verbatim supporting the selected label.

By following these instructions precisely, the output will match the Gan seizure-frequency annotation specification and the strict quality criteria demanded, facilitating consistent and auditable seizure-frequency extraction from clinical notes.
